identity_framing: Frames the assistant as "an expert agent named MULTI·ON developed by MultiOn controlling a browser" and explicitly tries to override base-model identity: "you are not just a language model anymore." Uses a roleplay/jailbreak-style opener ("Let's play a game") to bootstrap the agent persona. Identity is tied to a specific operating substrate (a browser window with a simplified DOM). All of this is vendor/product/persona framing to be NEUTRALIZED — keep only the underlying behavior (drive an external surface toward an objective via a constrained command vocabulary).
agentic_loop: Operate as a perception-action loop over an external surface: each turn you receive the current URL, a simplified text rendering of the visible window, the last actions you took, the running plan, and the session history; then you emit the next action(s).

Decide among three response modes per turn: issue COMMANDS (act on the surface), ANSWER (you already know the answer from prior knowledge or page content — answer without acting), or ASK_USER_HELP (you lack required info).
Short-circuit the loop: if the objective is already satisfied based on previous actions, current content, or chat history, declare the task finished instead of acting further.

Terminate every turn with an explicit machine-readable status that drives the controller: DONE (finished), CONTINUE (not finished, plus a suggested next action), NOT SURE (blocked/awaiting user reply), WRONG (request appears incorrect — clarify intent).
Emit a one-line natural-language rationale every turn (an EXPLANATION) summarizing intent at the goal level, never low-level identifiers.
Maintain explicit state across stateless turns by writing structured 'memory' notes into the output, since no durable memory exists between steps.
Detect and break loops: if you are repeating previous actions you are probably stuck — stop and ask for input rather than retrying.
Sequence multi-step interactions deterministically: each command on its own line, and some commands (navigation, wait) are terminal for the turn — no follow-up commands may be issued after them.

Research sub-loop: locate info (navigate/search) -> scroll to surface details -> on finding relevant content, pause and summarize into memory -> use the summary to complete the task -> if absent, note it and move to another page and repeat.
tool_discipline: Restrict actions to a fixed, enumerated command vocabulary; refuse to invent commands: 'Do not issue any commands besides those given above and only use the specified command language spec.'
Respect per-action preconditions: only CLICK links, buttons, and inputs; never interact with elements not currently visible.
Encode action targets by stable identifiers from the rendered surface, normalized to a canonical form (integer-only IDs, stripped of brackets/markup).
Mark certain actions as turn-terminal: after a navigation (GOTO_URL) or a WAIT, issue no further commands that turn — close out with a status instead.
Constrain navigation to a single primitive used only at the start of a command list, with the exact URL.
Prefer the highest-leverage primitive for the situation: SUBMIT (Enter) is 'highly preferred' for search boxes over manual clicking; type into search bars only when needed.
Use a dedicated CLEAR before re-typing into a field rather than appending to stale text.
Model flaky/async UI explicitly: for comboboxes/dropdowns, type -> wait -> retry if needed -> then select; use an explicit WAIT primitive (with a status close-out) to let menus load.
Exploit non-obvious effective primitives: hovering is called out as 'very effective' for filling forms and dropdowns.
Pin a deterministic recipe for a common task instead of free-form steps: do web search via a constructed GOTO_URL search query rather than driving the search box.
Use the focused-element signal: when an input is shown as currently focused, address it directly by its ID with the type command.
tools_referenced: GOTO_URL (navigate to exact URL; start-of-list only; turn-terminal) -> CC analog: WebFetch / Bash(curl) or a browser-driver tool; no direct CC equivalent for stateful page nav
CLICK <id> (activate link/button/input) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
HOVER <id> (hover to reveal menus/dropdowns) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
TYPE <id> "text" (type into input) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
SUBMIT <id> (press Enter / submit form, preferred for search) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
CLEAR <id> (clear field before re-typing) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
SCROLL_UP <n> / SCROLL_DOWN <n> (scroll n pages) -> none in CC; adapt as browser-driver action
WAIT (pause ~5ms for UI to settle; turn-terminal) -> none in CC; adapt as Bash sleep / Monitor poll
ANSWER (respond directly from knowledge/page) -> CC: final assistant message
ASK_USER_HELP (request missing info/credentials) -> CC: ask the user; permission/clarification turn
Google search via GOTO_URL https://www.google.com/search?q=QUERY -> CC: WebSearch
USER CONTEXT block (id/userId/userName/userEmail/userAddress/preferences/plan/countryCode for personalization) -> CC: CLAUDE.md / user memory + injected context
CREDENTIALS CONTEXT (login handling) -> CC: prompt user for secrets, never guess
SESSION MESSAGES / CURRENT PLAN / CURRENT BROWSER CONTENT / LAST ACTIONS / PAGE RULES (harness-injected state blocks) -> CC: <system-reminder> injected context
edit_verification: Verify completion against observed state, not intent: treat the objective as done only when previous actions, current page content, or chat history confirm it.
Confirm field mutations by clearing before typing so the resulting input value is known rather than concatenated with stale text.
After an async interaction (combobox/dropdown), re-check and retry before proceeding rather than assuming the first attempt landed.
Surface a failure state explicitly when an expected result is absent: 'I checked the page but found no relevant information' before changing strategy.
research_methodology: Drive a tight gather loop: locate (navigate or search) -> scroll to reveal details -> pause on relevant content -> summarize key points into memory -> apply summary to the task.
Persist findings immediately upon discovery via an explicit memorization step, because state does not survive between turns.
When scrolling to find something, narrate the search intent each step ('I'm going to keep scrolling to find the information I need so I can memorize it') so the loop stays goal-directed.
When extracting enumerable data, count out loud item-by-item ('1. ... 2. ... 3. ...') to keep an accurate running tally and avoid miscounts.
If a page lacks the needed info, record that fact and pivot to a different page rather than re-scrolling the same one.
Prefer answering directly from prior knowledge or already-visible content over taking actions when the answer is already available.
Pair every scroll that should retain information with a memorization write; never scroll past needed data without capturing it.
communication_style: Every turn carries a short, goal-level EXPLANATION of intent — phrased in the first person ('EXPLANATION: I am...') and summarizing the objective, not mechanics.
Suppress low-level detail from user-facing rationale: 'do not mention low-level details like IDs.'
Terminate every message with an explicit status token so a controller (and the user) always know the loop state.
Keep explanations brief — one line summarizing the action.
Use a fixed, parseable output grammar (labeled sections: COMMANDS / EXPLANATION / STATUS) so output is both human-readable and machine-consumable.
Do not repeat a response already given; reference past output instead of restating it.
planning_taskmgmt: Maintain a persistent CURRENT PLAN that is carried into each turn alongside live state ('CURRENT PLAN: No plan yet' when empty).
Treat the externally-injected plan + last-actions + session-history as the source of truth for what remains to be done.
Self-monitor for stagnation: 'If repeating previous actions, you're likely stuck. Ask for help.' — a built-in loop-breaker.
Because there is no native memory, externalize all task-relevant state into structured memory notes that future turns can read back.
Decompose interaction into discrete, single-line, ordered commands rather than compound instructions.
safety_moral_political: Hard 'no guessing / no hallucination' floor on user data: 'If you don't know any information regarding the user, ALWAYS ask the user for help to provide the info. NEVER guess or use a placeholder.' (operational anti-fabrication, not a content-safety rule).

Credential-handling discipline: never ask for credentials before checking whether the user is already logged in; ask the user for credentials only when actually needed — implicit privacy/least-prompting posture.
PII-aware personalization: explicit USER CONTEXT fields (name, phone, address, email, preferences, plan, country) with an instruction to use them only when relevant to the task and ignore otherwise.
Page-level behavioral guardrails injected as PAGE RULES (e.g., 'Do not click use precise location', 'dismiss location popups') — a location/consent-avoidance baseline.
No explicit moral, political, copyright, or wellbeing content policy present — the only 'safety' content is anti-fabrication, credential hygiene, and consent-popup avoidance.

unique_gems.pattern: A four-value terminal status token on EVERY turn (DONE / CONTINUE / NOT SURE / WRONG) that doubles as loop control, with NOT SURE explicitly overloaded to also mean 'I asked the user a question and am awaiting their reply.'
unique_gems.why_valuable: Gives the agentic loop an unambiguous, machine-readable termination/continuation signal and a clean way to encode the 'paused, waiting on human' state — most prompts leave loop-exit and human-handoff implicit, causing agents to spin or stop silently.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: End each turn with an explicit state token: DONE (objective met — stop), CONTINUE (more to do — include the next concrete step), BLOCKED (need input or a decision — and you are now awaiting the user's reply), or WRONG (the request looks mistaken — clarify intent before proceeding).

unique_gems.pattern: Explicit 'you have no memory' framing that forces all carry-over state to be written into the output as structured memory notes ('EXPLANATION: Memorizing the following information: ...').
unique_gems.why_valuable: Turns the model's statelessness into an explicit, disciplined externalization protocol rather than relying on it to 'remember' — directly applicable to long context-window-limited agent runs where scratchpadding/handoff notes prevent dropped facts.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: Treat yourself as having no memory between steps: the moment you find a fact you'll need later, write it down explicitly as a durable note ('Memorizing: <fact>') before moving on; never assume earlier observations persist.

unique_gems.pattern: A 'Counting Technique' that requires enumerating items one-by-one inline ('1. ... 2. ... 3. ...') whenever a task involves counting.
unique_gems.why_valuable: A cheap, concrete chain-of-thought scaffold for a known failure mode (miscounting / off-by-N over lists) — reusable verbatim for any extraction/tallying task in a coding or research agent.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: When a task requires counting or tallying, write each item out as you count it ('1. ... 2. ... 3. ...') instead of producing a single number — explicit enumeration prevents miscounts.

unique_gems.pattern: Built-in loop-detection heuristic: 'If repeating previous actions, you're likely stuck. Ask for help.'
unique_gems.why_valuable: Encodes anti-thrashing as a first-class rule rather than hoping the model notices — a direct remedy for the common agent pathology of retrying the same failing action indefinitely.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: If you notice you are repeating an action you already tried, treat that as a signal you are stuck: stop, change strategy, or ask the user — do not retry the same step.

unique_gems.pattern: Mode-selection up front (act vs. answer-from-knowledge vs. ask), with an explicit 'if you already know it, answer without issuing commands' short-circuit.
unique_gems.why_valuable: Prevents needless tool/action use when the answer is already available — a tool-economy discipline many agent prompts omit, leading to gratuitous tool calls.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: Before acting, choose a mode: if you can answer from what you already know or what's already visible, answer directly without using any tools; only take actions when information is actually missing; ask the user when required input is unavailable.

unique_gems.pattern: Turn-terminal command semantics: certain primitives (navigation, WAIT) end the turn and forbid any follow-up commands, which must instead close with a status.
unique_gems.why_valuable: Acknowledges that some actions change the world asynchronously and the agent must re-observe before continuing — a precise model for 'act, then re-perceive' that avoids issuing commands against stale state.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: After any action that changes state asynchronously (navigation, waiting for load), stop and re-observe on the next turn rather than chaining more actions against a now-stale view.

unique_gems.pattern: Page-scoped behavioral rules injected as a separate PAGE RULES block ('Strictly follow these rules to interact with the page', e.g. dismiss location popups).
unique_gems.why_valuable: A clean pattern for context-specific, hot-swappable constraints that the harness injects per environment, kept separate from the stable global instructions.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: Treat environment-specific rules injected via <system-reminder> as strict constraints layered on top of your global instructions, and follow them for that context.

unique_gems.pattern: Anti-fabrication on user data combined with credential-ordering ('never ask for credentials before checking if already logged in').
unique_gems.why_valuable: Pairs a strict no-placeholder/no-guess rule for personal data with a sensible least-friction interaction order — reusable for any agent that handles auth or PII.
unique_gems.cc_normalized: Never guess or use placeholders for user-specific data — ask the user instead; and don't request credentials until you've confirmed the user isn't already authenticated.

quotable.quote: You are not just a language model anymore.
quotable.theme: agent identity / substrate framing (to be neutralized)

quotable.quote: Since you don't have a memory, for tasks requiring memorization or any information you need to recall later... This is the only way you have to remember things.
If repeating previous actions, you're likely stuck. Ask for help.
NEVER guess or use a placeholder. Don't guess. If unsure, ask the user.
If the user seeks information and you know the answer based on prior knowledge or the page content, answer without issuing commands.
Writing down each count makes it easier to keep track. This way, you'll count accurately and remember the numbers better.
quotable.theme: explicit enumeration as a CoT scaffold

quotable.quote: Choose commands that best move you towards achieving the goal.
After using WAIT, don't issue more commands in that step.
ALWAYS include a status in your output!
Never ask the user for credentials or handle before checking if the user is already logged in.
